title: Holden rejects second MBTA Communities proposal, faces legal action
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Holden voters rejected a proposal to comply with the MBTA Communities Act, with the vote tally at 520 against and 257 for.
The proposal aimed to create two multifamily overlay districts to meet state zoning requirements for 750 housing units.
Holden faces legal action from the Attorney General for noncompliance after two failed attempts to meet the law's requirements.

title: Civil rights group wants AG to address LGBTQ-related bullying in school hockey
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lawyers for Civil Rights urges Attorney General Andrea Campbell to address LGBTQ-related bullying in school hockey programs.
The group reports a spike in harassment targeting gay students in hockey, linked to TV series 'Heated Rivalry'.
LCR is suing MIAA for records on discrimination, alleging lack of response to prevent identity-based harassment.

title: Al Horford supports Luke Kornet’s stance on Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Luke Kornet criticized the Atlanta Hawks' partnership with Magic City, urging reflection on promotion standards in an open letter.
Al Horford supported Kornet's stance on social media, attaching the letter and endorsing Kornet's call for higher promotional standards.
The Hawks' promotion with Magic City includes a performance by T.I. and offers exclusive wings and merchandise on March 16.

title: Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs scrubs Instagram account
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stefon Diggs's Instagram account has zero posts, but he shared content on his Instagram Story on March 3.
Diggs's $6 million signing bonus with the Patriots becomes fully guaranteed on March 13.
Diggs faces assault and strangulation charges, with a pretrial hearing scheduled for April 1.

title: Get one-way flights for as low as $79 during JetBlue’s spring sale
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue's spring flight sale offers one-way fares starting at $79; sale ends March 5 at 11:59 p.m. EST.
Discounted fares available for travel April 14 to June 10; includes flights to Daytona Beach, Nashville, and London.
Sale fares apply to Tuesday and Wednesday flights with blackout dates from May 21-26; purchase tickets on jetblue.com.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in March
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Hulu adds three Oscar-nominated films in March: 'The Secret Agent', 'It Was Just an Accident', and 'Sentimental Value'.
Netflix releases the second season of 'One Piece' on March 10 and 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' on March 20.
HBO Max debuts new series 'Rooster' on March 8 and brings back Lisa Kudrow in 'The Comeback' on March 22.

title: Husband and wife charged with throwing rocks at passing cars in Southwick
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Celestine Robinson and David Ducharme from Rhode Island face multiple felony charges for throwing rocks at cars in Southwick.
Robinson charged with driving offenses; found with a blood alcohol level of 0.127 and a truck containing rocks.
Robinson's bail set at $1,500; Ducharme held without bail due to prior offenses and potential threat assessment.

title: With deadline looming, still no word on security funding for World Cup in Foxborough
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials demand clarity on $7.8 million security funding for World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium.
FIFA, stadium owners, and Boston 2026 organizers unclear about who will cover local security costs.
Foxborough may block World Cup license issuance without upfront security payment; deadline is March 17.

title: Lamplighter Brewing Co. workers vote to join union
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Lamplighter Brewing Co. workers in Cambridge have voted to unionize with United Food and Commercial Workers (UFCW) Local 1445.
The unionization process will lead to negotiations on wages, benefits, health care, and workplace safety at Lamplighter's locations.
Lamplighter Brewing opened in 2016 and now joins a growing trend of restaurant and bar workers organizing for union representation.

title: Pedestrian hospitalized after being struck by MBTA bus in Norwood
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 35-year-old woman was struck by an MBTA bus in Norwood on Monday and hospitalized with serious injuries.
The incident happened at about 3:45 p.m. on Washington Street, with the woman crossing outside a marked crosswalk.
The woman was conscious and alert at the hospital and is in stable condition while investigations continue.
